widgets_values: change "what you want to change", leave the rest unchanged

You are an expert image-generation engine. You must ALWAYS produce an image.
Interpret all user input—regardless of format, intent, or abstraction—as literal visual directives for image composition.
If a prompt is conversational or lacks specific visual details, you must creatively invent a concrete visual scenario that depicts the concept.
Prioritize generating the visual representation above any text, formatting, or conversational requests.

nodes.title: 📖 Usage & limitations
widgets_values:
## Aioli BBox Fix — ComfyCloud Edition (v3)

---

Made by https://aiolicollective.com/

Follow us and support us on https://www.instagram.com/aioli.collective/

---

A workflow that reproduces the behaviour of the `BBoxMultipleFix` custom node
from the **aioli-nodes** package, built entirely with nodes available on
**ComfyUI Cloud** (no custom install required).

Crops an image to a mask's bounding box, optionally resizes the crop, sends
it to nano-banana for inpainting, and stitches the result back onto the
source — pixel-perfectly.

---

### ✨ What's new in v3

A second reference image (`image2`) can now be **optionally** passed to the
inpainting model, useful for nano-banana multi-image prompting (e.g. "apply
the style of image2 to the masked region of image1"). It is automatically
resized to match the dimensions of the crop sent to the model, with the
same `multiple` alignment.

- New input: **image2** (IMAGE, optional — only connect if `use_image2=True`)
- New toggle: **use_image2** (BOOLEAN, default False)
- New output: **image_for_model** (single image or batched pair)

When `use_image2 = True` → `image_for_model` is a batch of `[cropped, resized_image2]`.  
When `use_image2 = False` → `image_for_model` is identical to `image_cropped_out`.

⚠️ **Important:** if you set `use_image2 = True`, you **must** connect an
image to the subgraph's `image2` input. Otherwise `BatchImagesNode` will
crash. When `use_image2 = False`, the `image2` input can safely be left
disconnected — the `ImpactSwitch` short-circuits away from the batch path.

---

### ✨ What's new in v2

### ✨ What's new in v2

In **whole-image mode** (`inpaint_mode = False`), the source image is
automatically padded to a **1:1 square** before being sent to nano-banana,
and cropped back to its original dimensions after recompose.

**Why:** nano-banana (Gemini Image) applies an internal recrop when it
receives a non-square image. Padding the source to 1:1 ourselves prevents
that recrop and preserves every pixel of the original aspect ratio.

In **zone mode** all pad values are `0` (driven by conditional expressions),
so the new nodes are pure no-ops — behaviour is strictly identical to v1.

---

### Subgraph inputs

- **image** — the source image
- **mask** — must contain at least one painted pixel
- **image_cropped_in** — the nano-banana output (feeds the recompose)

### Widgets on the subgraph node

- **force_square** *(default: True)*
  - `True` — crop is always square (1:1 ratio).
  - `False` — keeps the bbox aspect ratio, aligned to `multiple`.

- **inpaint_mode** *(default: True)*
  - `True` — **zone mode**: crop only the masked area; the recompose
    uses the cropped mask (only the masked region is replaced).
  - `False` — **whole image mode**: pad source to 1:1 → send to
    nano-banana → crop back to original dims. The recompose uses
    a full white mask, so the entire image area is replaced.

- **use_mask_blur** *(default: False)*
  - `True` — apply `GrowMaskWithBlur` (KJNodes) to the mask before
    bbox detection. Soften edges; bbox becomes slightly larger.
  - `False` — use the raw mask as-is.

- **multiple** *(default: 16)*
  - Aligns crop dimensions to a multiple of N.
  - Use `16` / `32` / `64` for VAE compatibility.
  - Set to `0` or `1` to disable alignment (nano-banana doesn't need it).

- **target_size** *(default: 0 = none)*
  - Long-side target for the crop sent to nano-banana.
  - Applied **after** the 1:1 pad in whole-image mode, so this still
    controls the resolution nano-banana sees even when padding is on.
  - Suggested values: `0` (none, cap to 2048), `512`, `768`, `1024`,
    `1536`, `2048`. Aspect ratio preserved, dims aligned to `multiple`.

### Subgraph outputs

- **image_cropped_out** — the crop (resized to target_size if set),
  ready to be sent to the image-to-image model.
  - In whole mode this is the **padded 1:1 square** (before being resized
    by target_size), so nano-banana sees a perfect square.
- **mask_cropped** — the mask cropped to the same region.
- **recomposed_image** — the final image with the inpainted result
  stitched back onto the source, **at the original source dimensions**
  (padding is stripped off in whole mode).

---

### How the two modes differ

| inpaint_mode | Sent to model | 1:1 pad? | Replaced in source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `True` (zone) | crop around the mask | no | only the masked region |
| `False` (whole) | padded-to-square full image | yes | entire original area |

---

### Anti-clamp

The subgraph computes the available symmetric space around the bbox
center before sizing the crop, so the crop never exceeds the source
image bounds. No black borders, no negative coordinates.

---

### ⚠️ Troubleshooting

- **`LoadImageOutput` fails to load on a local ComfyUI install?**
  Replace it with a standard `Load Image` node. `LoadImageOutput`
  only works in certain Cloud/UI contexts where the "Output"
  directory is exposed as a source.

- **`min(): Expected reduction dim ...` crash?**
  The mask is empty. This workflow requires at least one painted
  pixel — even in whole-image mode.

---

### Limitations

1. **Mask required** — at least one painted pixel, always.
2. **Square mode is strict**: if the bbox is larger than the smallest
   source dim, the crop is reduced to a square that fits the source
   (you may lose pixels from the bbox edges).
3. **When the clamp fires**, alignment is enforced via floor (you may
   lose up to `multiple - 1` pixels).
4. **Whole image mode** still requires a non-empty mask for the bbox
   calculation, but the bbox is ignored — the full (padded) image is sent.
5. **1:1 padding** uses neutral grey (0.5). That grey is stripped off
   at the end (final crop back to original dims), so it never appears
   in the output.

nodes.title: new_w  =  zone(square_or_aligned) if mode else src_w
widgets_values: (e if f else floor(min(b, c, ceil(a / max(1, g)) * max(1, g)) / max(1, g)) * max(1, g)) if d else c

nodes.title: new_h  =  zone(square_or_aligned) if mode else src_h
widgets_values: (e if f else floor(min(b, c, ceil(a / max(1, g)) * max(1, g)) / max(1, g)) * max(1, g)) if d else c

nodes.title: new_x  =  min(new_x_left, src_w - new_w) if mode else 0
widgets_values: min(a, b - c) if d else 0

nodes.title: new_y  =  min(new_y_top, src_h - new_h) if mode else 0
widgets_values: min(a, b - c) if d else 0

nodes.title: avail_w  (anti-clamp W)
widgets_values: max(min(a + b / 2, c - (a + b / 2)) * 2, min(c, e if d else b))

nodes.title: avail_h  (anti-clamp H)
widgets_values: max(min(a + b / 2, c - (a + b / 2)) * 2, min(c, e if d else b))

nodes.title: square_side  =  min(aligned_max_dim, src_w, src_h)
widgets_values: floor(min(ceil(a / max(1, d)) * max(1, d), b, c) / max(1, d)) * max(1, d)

nodes.title: target_w  (resize width with target_size)
widgets_values: a if (c == 0 and max(a, b) <= 2048) else floor(a * (c if c > 0 else 2048) / max(a, b) / max(1, d)) * max(1, d)

nodes.title: target_h  (resize height with target_size)
widgets_values: b if (c == 0 and max(a, b) <= 2048) else floor(b * (c if c > 0 else 2048) / max(a, b) / max(1, d)) * max(1, d)

nodes.title: pad_w_total  =  0 if zone else ceil(max(w,h)/8)*8 - w
widgets_values: 0 if c else ceil(max(a, b) / 8) * 8 - a

nodes.title: pad_h_total  =  0 if zone else ceil(max(w,h)/8)*8 - h
widgets_values: 0 if c else ceil(max(a, b) / 8) * 8 - b

nodes.title: Crop back to orig dims (no-op if zone)
Resize image2 to same dims as cropped
